sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: RSC Anderlecht are in strong form with 3 wins and no losses in their last 5 matches, including 3 clean sheets. KVC Westerlo have had mixed results, with 3 wins and 2 losses in their last 5, often conceding goals as they have no clean sheets in 8 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, KVC Westerlo won the most recent encounter 2-0, while RSC Anderlecht dominated the prior match with a 4-1 victory at home. Historically, matches between these teams often see over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Streaks
sections.text: Anderlecht have been dominant at home, scoring first in their last 7 matches and winning the first half in 5. Westerlo struggle defensively, conceding in all their last 8 games, with matches often featuring over 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Additional Factors
sections.text: The referee averages 4 yellow cards per game, aligning with both teams' streak of under 4.5 cards in recent matches. Corner stats suggest fewer than 10.5 corners for Anderlecht games, which could influence the match dynamics.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Lotto Park saw RSC Anderlecht take a commanding lead over KVC Westerlo with a 2-0 scoreline. Anderlecht capitalized on their opportunities with clinical finishing, as goals from Thorgan Hazard and Tristan Degreef showcased their attacking prowess. Westerlo, despite having more ball possession, struggled to convert their control into meaningful chances, failing to register a shot on target. The home side demonstrated greater efficiency in the final third, creating big chances and converting them effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The game began with Westerlo dictating the early play, showing consistent attacking intent in the opening minutes. However, momentum shifted dramatically around the midway point of the half when Anderlecht started to assert themselves with a series of attacking spells. From this point, the home team dominated with sustained pressure, frequently pushing forward in waves. Westerlo had brief moments of resurgence, especially towards the end of the half, but lacked the cutting edge to break through Anderlecht's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Anderlecht, Thorgan Hazard was a standout, not only scoring but also contributing to the team's attacking dynamism. Tristan Degreef also made a significant impact with his well-taken goal, while Nilson Angulo impressed with his playmaking ability, setting up opportunities. On the other side, Westerlo's players struggled to make an impact, with no one managing to test the Anderlecht goalkeeper. Josimar Alcócer missed a big chance, highlighting their inability to capitalize on the limited opportunities they created.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Anderlecht's dominance in the penalty area was evident, with far more entries and possessions won in the final third compared to Westerlo. Their ability to create big chances was a key differentiator, even though they missed one themselves. Westerlo, despite holding a higher percentage of possession and winning more duels, failed to translate this into dangerous situations, often being stifled before reaching threatening areas. Their aerial strength was notable, but it did little to trouble Anderlecht's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Anderlecht's approach in the first half was focused on quick transitions and exploiting spaces in Westerlo's defense, which paid off with their two goals. Their defensive structure remained solid, limiting Westerlo to minimal chances despite the away side's possession advantage. Westerlo, on the other hand, seemed to lack a clear plan in the final third, often resorting to hopeful plays rather than structured attacks. They will need to adjust their strategy to break through Anderlecht's compact setup in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Anderlecht are likely to continue their aggressive approach, aiming to extend their lead while maintaining a solid defensive shape. Westerlo must take risks to get back into the game, potentially leaving gaps at the back which Anderlecht could exploit. The away side will need to be more direct and improve their finishing to have any chance of a comeback. Given the current dynamics, it seems probable that Anderlecht will maintain control, but Westerlo might find a way to pull one back if they can capitalize on any lapse in concentration.
